sources.exact_document_title: British Association of Dermatologists guidelines for the management of people with vitiligo 2021
sources.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; specialty guidance requiring UAE adaptation
sources.population: Adults and children with clinician-established or suspected vitiligo; the guideline explicitly does not cover diagnosis.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary care and specialist dermatology assessment and follow-up.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for documenting site, type, extent, stability, speed, triggers, quality-of-life and psychosocial impact, autoimmune history, and medical photography. It is not used to diagnose vitiligo or generate tests, treatment, medicine, referral, or follow-up intervals.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations R1, R3, and R5 — history, extent, stability, impact, and associated disease
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting lesion site and distribution, clinician-established type, extent, stability, speed, triggers, quality-of-life and psychosocial impact, and personal or family autoimmune and thyroid context.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendation R8 — photography and monitoring
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording medical photography and documented affected areas for clinician-led monitoring without creating a response conclusion or fixed interval.

limitations: bad-vitiligo-guideline-2021: full source unavailable during reconstruction
